name: Everest Base Camp
description: The world's most iconic trek. Fly into Lukla and trek through Sherpa villages, rhododendron forests, and glacial moraines to reach the foot of the world's highest peak. The journey includes a visit to Kala Patthar (5,644m) for sunrise views of Everest, Lhotse, and Nuptse.

name: Annapurna Circuit
description: Nepal's most diverse trek, circling the Annapurna massif. Cross the Thorong La Pass (5,416m), explore the Mustang region's Tibetan Buddhist culture, and descend through the world's deepest gorge. Unmatched variety of landscapes from subtropical forests to high desert.

name: Annapurna Base Camp
description: A stunning trek into the heart of the Annapurna Sanctuary. Walk through lush rhododendron forests and traditional Gurung villages to reach the base camp surrounded by a amphitheater of peaks including Annapurna I, Machhapuchhre, and Hiunchuli.

name: Langtang Valley
description: The closest Himalayan trek from Kathmandu. Journey through the beautiful Langtang Valley, home to the indigenous Tamang people. The trail offers spectacular mountain views, yak pastures, and the opportunity to climb Kyanjin Ri for panoramic vistas.

name: Manaslu Circuit
description: A restricted area trek offering pristine wilderness around the world's eighth highest peak. Requires a registered guide. The circuit crosses the Larkya La Pass (5,106m) and offers a more remote alternative to the Annapurna Circuit with rich Buddhist culture.

name: Kanchenjunga
description: One of Nepal's most remote and challenging treks, circling the third highest mountain in the world. Requires a registered guide. The route passes through pristine forests, remote villages, and offers breathtaking views of Kanchenjunga's massive south face.

name: Dolpo
description: The legendary 'Hidden Valley' made famous by Peter Matthiessen's 'The Snow Leopard'. Upper Dolpo requires a restricted area permit with a licensed guide. Explore ancient Tibetan Buddhist culture, crystal-clear Phoksundo Lake, and the stark beauty of the Trans-Himalayan desert.

name: Mera Peak
description: Nepal's highest trekking peak at 6,476m. A technical climb requiring mountaineering experience and proper gear (crampons, ice axe, harness). Offers unparalleled views of five 8,000m peaks: Everest, Lhotse, Makalu, Cho Oyu, and Kanchenjunga.

name: Island Peak
description: A popular trekking peak in the Everest region. The climb involves glacier travel, a steep headwall, and a final summit ridge with incredible views of Lhotse, Nuptse, and Everest. Requires prior climbing experience.

name: Kathmandu Valley
alt_name: Kathmandu Durbar Square, Pashupatinath, Boudhanath, Swayambhunath
description: The cultural heart of Nepal, home to seven UNESCO World Heritage Sites within a 15km radius. Explore ancient durbar squares, sacred Hindu temples at Pashupatinath, the massive Boudhanath stupa, and the monkey temple at Swayambhunath with panoramic valley views.

name: Lumbini
description: The sacred birthplace of Lord Buddha, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Walk through the Maya Devi Temple, the Sacred Garden, and explore monasteries built by Buddhist communities from around the world. A deeply peaceful and spiritual destination.

name: Bandipur
description: A beautifully preserved Newari hill town perched on a ridge overlooking the Marsyangdi Valley. Known for its traditional architecture, stunning mountain views, and laid-back atmosphere. Perfect for a cultural escape between Kathmandu and Pokhara.

name: Panauti
description: A historic Newari town at the confluence of the Roshi and Punyamati rivers. Known for its three sacred rivers, 16th-century temples, and traditional architecture. A peaceful alternative to crowded Kathmandu with rich cultural heritage.

name: Bhaktapur Durbar Square
description: The best-preserved medieval city in Nepal, a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Walk through ancient alleys lined with brick houses, visit the 55-Window Palace, the Nyatapola Temple, and taste the famous king curd (juju dhau).

name: Chitwan National Park
description: Nepal's first national park and a UNESCO World Heritage Site. Home to the one-horned rhino, Bengal tiger, and over 500 bird species. Enjoy jeep safaris, canoe rides, and guided jungle walks through dense sal forests and grasslands.

name: Bardia National Park
description: Nepal's largest and most remote national park, offering a true wilderness experience. With a fraction of Chitwan's visitors, Bardia provides intimate wildlife encounters including tigers, elephants, crocodiles, and Gangetic dolphins in the Karnali River.

name: Koshi Tappu Wildlife Reserve
description: Nepal's largest bird sanctuary and a Ramsar site on the Saptakoshi River floodplain. A paradise for bird watchers with over 485 species including the endangered swamp francolin and Bengal florican. Also home to wild water buffalo and Gangetic dolphins.

name: Pokhara
description: Nepal's adventure capital nestled beside Phewa Lake with spectacular views of the Annapurna range. The launching point for most Annapurna treks. Offers paragliding, zip-lining, ultralight flights, kayaking, and peaceful lakeside relaxation.

name: Ghorepani Poon Hill
description: Nepal's most popular short trek, renowned for the spectacular sunrise view from Poon Hill (3,210m). The panoramic vista takes in Dhaulagiri, Annapurna, Machhapuchhre, and over 20 other Himalayan peaks. Perfect for beginners and families.

name: Sarangkot
description: The premier viewpoint above Pokhara, famous for paragliding takeoffs and unparalleled sunrise views of the Annapurna and Dhaulagiri ranges. A short drive or hike from Pokhara. Also the starting point for the Sarangkot to Pame trek.

name: Nagarkot
description: A popular hill station just outside Kathmandu, famous for its sunrise view of the Himalayas spanning from Everest in the east to Annapurna in the west. Offers easy hiking trails and a peaceful escape from the city.

name: Ghandruk
description: A beautiful Gurung village on the Annapurna trekking route known for its traditional stone houses, museum, and spectacular views of Annapurna South, Machhapuchhre, and Hiunchuli. A perfect cultural stop on any Annapurna itinerary.

name: Rara Lake
description: Nepal's largest lake (10.8 sq km) at 2,990m, nestled in the remote Karnali region. The deep blue waters surrounded by pine forests and snow-capped peaks make it one of the most beautiful yet least visited destinations in Nepal. A true hidden gem.

name: Upper Mustang
description: The ancient Buddhist kingdom of Lo Manthang, a restricted area that requires a licensed guide and significant permit fees. The landscape resembles the Tibetan plateau — arid, wind-sculpted cliffs, ancient caves, and whitewashed monasteries. A journey through living history.

name: Nar Phu Valley
description: A remote restricted area trek combining pristine landscapes with authentic Tibetan Buddhist culture. Explore the hidden valleys of Nar and Phu, cross the challenging Kang La Pass (5,320m), and visit ancient monasteries untouched by modern tourism.

name: Tsum Valley
description: Known as the 'Hidden Valley of Happiness', Tsum Valley is a sacred Buddhist pilgrimage site rich in ancient monasteries, mani walls, and chortens. The valley's isolation has preserved Tibetan Buddhist culture in its purest form. Can be combined with the Manaslu Circuit.

name: Humla
description: Nepal's most remote and isolated region in the far northwest, bordering Tibet. The trek to Limi Valley offers a true expedition experience with ancient Bon and Tibetan Buddhist traditions, the remote village of Simikot, and views of Mount Kailash across the border.

name: Janakpur
description: The historic capital of the ancient Mithila kingdom and birthplace of Goddess Sita. Janakpur is home to the magnificent Janaki Mandir, a stunning neo-classical temple dedicated to Sita. The city comes alive during the Vivah Panchami festival, reenacting the wedding of Rama and Sita with grand processions and celebrations. The Mithila art form, with its intricate paintings, originated here.

name: Tansen
description: A historic Newari hill town perched on a ridge in the Mahabharat range. Tansen offers stunning views of the Himalayas, well-preserved traditional architecture, and the magnificent Tansen Durbar. Known for its metalwork, dhaka fabric, and peaceful atmosphere. The town is a gateway to the Ridi Bazaar and the Kali Gandaki River.

name: Ilam
description: Nepal's premier tea-growing region in the eastern hills, famously called the 'Queen of Hills'. Ilam's rolling green tea estates, misty mornings, and views of Kanchenjunga make it a photographer's paradise. Visit the iconic Kanyam tea gardens, hike to the Mai Pokhari lake, and taste some of Nepal's finest orthodox teas.

name: Gorkha
description: The historic birthplace of King Prithvi Narayan Shah, the unifier of Nepal. Perched on a hilltop, Gorkha Durbar is a stunning palace-fortress with panoramic Himalayan views. The town is the spiritual home of the famous Gorkha soldiers. A perfect day trip from Kathmandu or stop between Pokhara and Kathmandu.

name: Dhulikhel
description: A charming Newari town on the Arnico Highway, just 30km from Kathmandu. Dhulikhel offers spectacular Himalayan panoramas stretching from Annapurna to Everest without the crowds of Nagarkot. Explore the ancient Newari settlement, hike to Namobuddha Monastery, and enjoy the tranquility of the surrounding hills.

name: Jomsom
description: The gateway to Upper Mustang and the largest settlement in the Mustang region. Jomsom is famous for the powerful Muktinath winds, apple orchards, and dramatic Trans-Himalayan landscape. Accessible by flight from Pokhara, it's the starting point for treks to Muktinath, Upper Mustang, and the Annapurna Circuit.

name: Tatopani
description: A popular stop on the Annapurna Circuit, famous for its natural hot springs on the banks of the Kali Gandaki River. Literally meaning 'hot water' in Nepali, Tatopani is the perfect place to soak tired trekking muscles in therapeutic mineral baths surrounded by Himalayan scenery.

name: Hetauda
description: A rapidly growing city at the crossroads of Kathmandu, Chitwan, and Birgunj. Hetauda is known for its scenic setting in the Rapti Valley, the nearby Martyr's Memorial Park with its botanical gardens, and the scenic hike up to the Churia Hills. A pleasant stopover with easy access to the Maulakalika Temple.

name: Dhankuta
description: The administrative headquarters of eastern Nepal, perched on a ridge with breathtaking views of the Himalayas from Everest to Kanchenjunga. Dhankuta is famous for its scenic landscape, the historic Dhankuta Bazaar, and as the starting point for treks to the Arun Valley and Makalu region.

name: Nuwakot
description: A historic trading town and the former capital of the Shah kings before the unification of Nepal. The seven-storey Nuwakot Durbar Palace, built in the Malla architectural style, is one of the finest examples of traditional Nepali palace architecture. A peaceful day trip from Kathmandu with stunning valley views.

name: Syangja
description: A scenic hill town overlooking the Seti River Valley, known for its panoramic Himalayan views and terraced farmlands. Syangja is famous for the Dhapuk Sera viewpoint, the Sidhha Gufa (one of Nepal's largest caves), and its peaceful rural atmosphere away from tourist crowds.

name: Butwal
description: A bustling city at the junction of the hills and the Terai plains, serving as the gateway to Pokhara and Lumbini. Butwal is known for the sacred Siddhababa Temple, the nearby Jitgadi Fort, and its position on the historic Mahendra Highway. A convenient stop with beautiful sunset views over the hills.

name: Kakani
description: A serene hill station just 25km northwest of Kathmandu, offering some of the closest Himalayan views from the capital. Kakani's strawberry farms are famous, and the ridge walk provides spectacular panoramas of the Ganesh, Langtang, and Manaslu ranges. A perfect weekend escape from Kathmandu.
